channel.title: Pages on My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on My New Hugo Project

item.title: ETB AAV LayerZero Framework
item.description:
The Problem

The cybersecurity industry is drowning in reactive patching. Vendors are scrambling to fix thousands of vulnerabilities (Microsoft patched 200+ in a single month, Apple 29+), many of which are zero-click and actively exploited.

A hardcoded architectural backdoor was discovered across multiple critical infrastructure vendors (Rockwell, Siemens, Ubiquiti, and Google/Android), proving the existence of a systemic, cross-industry failure in how trust is validated.

The Solution: ETB AAV LayerZero

The Eternal Trust Boundary (ETB) Authenticate, Authorize, Validate (AAV) LayerZero Framework is a proactive, pre-execution validation gate that sits at the network boundary.

item.title: White Paper: The Universal Backdoor & The Proactive Defense
item.description:
Executive Summary

A hardcoded, cross-vendor architectural backdoor (/) has been discovered affecting critical infrastructure. This flaw allows remote, zero-click authentication bypass across ATMs, industrial control systems (Rockwell, Siemens), and mobile ecosystems (Google/Android).

Traditional reactive patching has failed. The industry is facing a “patch tsunami” of 1,000+ vulnerabilities in a single cycle, with patches being bypassed or incomplete.

The ETB AAV LayerZero Framework

This patent-pending framework offers the only known proactive defense.
